title: Meliosma glabrata

This family is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as the angiosperms. The species Meliosma glabrata is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, and is characterized by its distinctive leaves and flowers. The classification of Meliosma glabrata reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Sabiaceae family.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.
